creator: _jkrow
caption:
This underrated Caribbean vacation is only a 4.5 hour flight from NYC ✈️ 

📍 @alaiabelize on Ambergris Caye, Belize. Say ‘Alaia’ and I’ll send the info to your inbox

We love Belize and were so excited to relax for a bit on Ambergris Caye. We flew into BZE, hopped on a 15 minute puddle jumper to San Pedro, and checked into
Alaia, a beachfront resort with a rooftop pool, great food and beautiful views.

Here’s a sample itinerary if you’re planning a trip:

✈️ Airport: Philip S.W. Goldson International (BZE) + Tropic Air hopper to San Pedro (SPR)

⏰ Time Needed: 4–5 days. Piggyback with a weekend and you’ll only need to use 2-3 days of
PTO!

🌴 Itinerary Highlights:
Day 1:
• Fly to San Pedro + check in at Alaia Belize
• Sunset sushi at Vista Rooftop

Day 2:
• Morning yoga + beach breakfast
• Rent a golf cart from the front desk at Alaia and head to Secret Beach. On the way back,
browse the San Pedro Artisan Market and grab lunch at the local food trucks

Day 3:
• Relax in a poolside cabana or book a diving or snorkeling excursion to Hol Chan Marine
Reserve
• Treat yourself to a spa treatment at K’in Spa (I highly recommend the deep tissue
massage!)
• Sign up for mixology class or grab dinner at The Deck Restaurant on site

Day 4:
• Blue Hole flyover. If you’re going to splurge on one thing, make it this! (as long as you’re
okay with very small planes)
• Book a sunset sail through Alaia to watch the sunset and maybe spot some dolphins
• Fancy dinner at Sea Salt Restaurant with live music

🌴 Is Belize on your radar? Say ‘Alaia’ and I’ll send you the info to book your stay!

transcription.text: Here's a PTO-friendly tropical getaway I bet you haven't thought of. This is Alaia, Belize, located on the beautiful island of Ambergris Cay off the coast of Belize. To get here, fly into Belize City and then hop on a puddle jumper to San Pedro. This is a little flight, but it's a fun start to your adventure because the views of the reef below are incredible. Once you get there, Alaia is right on the beach. They have three pools, five unique restaurants, which include sushi, seafood, and fine dining, and a variety of villas and spacious rooms with ocean views and balconies like this one. While you're here, treat yourself to a spa treatment, go to yoga, or relax in a poolside cabana. There are also lots of excursions that leave right from the resort like snorkeling in Scuba, sunset sails, and scenic flights over the iconic Great Blue Hole. If you splurge on one thing, make it this. You can also rent a golf cart right from the front desk to explore the rest of the island. Check out the artisan market and spend an afternoon swimming and sipping drinks in the water at Secret Beach. If you're looking for somewhere easy, romantic, and a little off the radar, then add Alaia to your list of ideas for your next getaway.
